title: quercus lineata var. thomsoniana
intro: Quercus lineata var. thomsoniana, also known as the Indian oak, is a deciduous tree species native to the Himalayas. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus lineata var. thomsoniana include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as its use in treating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its bark, leaves, and roots are commonly used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine.
